text.title: ModelEvaluator — Batch Evaluation Harness
text.summary: Core evaluation pipeline: EvaluationCase/EvaluationResult dataclasses, metric function protocol, aggregation with latency percentiles, and a CI quality gate pattern.

rule.statement: Define test cases as EvaluationCase(input, expected_output, metadata) and results as EvaluationResult(case, actual_output, metrics, latency_ms, tokens_used, error).
rule.rationale: Explicit dataclasses make metric functions stateless and testable independently of the evaluator loop.

rule.statement: Metric functions must have the signature (input: str, actual: str, expected: Optional[str]) -> Optional[float].
rule.rationale: Uniform signature lets any metric be added with `evaluator.add_metric(fn)` without modifying the evaluation loop.

rule.statement: Always set temperature=0 for all evaluation runs.
rule.rationale: Stochastic outputs make metric comparisons between runs unreliable; reproducibility requires deterministic decoding.

rule.statement: Wrap each API call in try/except and store the error in EvaluationResult.error; never abort the batch on a single failure.
rule.rationale: Network errors and API rate limits are expected; aborting loses all results collected so far.

rule.statement: Aggregate latency as mean, p50, and p95 — not just mean.
rule.rationale: Mean latency hides tail behavior; p95 is the metric users actually experience.

rule.statement: Compare aggregate metric means to pre-defined thresholds and return a pass/fail verdict with the full metric JSON.
rule.rationale: The CI system needs a boolean result, but the full JSON must be stored for trend analysis.

example.note: Temperature=0 is explicit. Returns structured dict with pass/fail and metric details.

antipattern.description: Running evaluation at temperature=1 (model default) and comparing results between runs.
antipattern.reason: Stochastic outputs produce different metric values each run; the comparison measures noise, not quality.

rule.statement: Use at least 100 test cases for any metric comparison used to make deployment decisions.
rule.rationale: Below 100 cases, metric rankings are statistically unreliable and reverse between runs due to sampling variance.

rule.statement: Include adversarial cases (edge cases, ambiguous instructions, long inputs) alongside representative cases.
rule.rationale: Easy cases produce artificially high metrics; adversarial cases reveal real failure modes.

rule.statement: Separate EvaluationCase datasets by task type (classification, QA, generation) and never mix in one aggregation run.
rule.rationale: Mixing task types makes metric aggregation meaningless — exact_match for classification and BLEU for generation are not comparable.

text.reference: Minimal CI gate with exact_match metric
